channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pathogenesis & Basic Science
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pathogenesis & Basic Science

item.title: The PVT1/miR-216a-5p/TLR4 Signaling Axis Regulates the Inflammation and Intestinal Epithelial Barrier Function of Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Journal of biochemical and molecular toxicology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic, relapsing disease, imposing a heavy disease burden and severely reducing the quality life of patients. This study verified the influence of the PVT1/miR-216a-5...

item.title: Microbiota-derived butyrate inhibits colonic epithelial pyroptosis and mitigates DSS-induced colitis via interacting with aryl hydrocarbon receptor.
item.description: Journal of translational medicine - Intestinal barrier defects cause antigen translocation and immune dysregulation. The pyroptosis of colonic epithelial cells (CECs) disrupts the colonic barrier, and its inhibition might be a therap...

item.title: Faecalibacterium prausnitzii-derived extracellular vesicles ameliorate experimental colitis through regulating barrier immunity and gut microbiota.
item.description: NPJ science of food - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is closely linked to intestinal barrier dysfunction and gut dysbiosis. Bacterial extracellular vesicles (bEVs) act as key mediators of bacteria-host crosstalk, with great po...

item.title: High-fat diet exacerbates experimental colitis by inhibiting lysosomal function via the STAT3-TFEB Axis.
item.description: Autophagy - An elevated risk for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) has been linked to the intake of high-fat diet (HFD), yet the underlying molecular mechanisms remain unclear. The lysosome and the macroautopha...

item.title: A novel peptide from Periplaneta americana: Development of a long-acting formulation for ulcerative colitis therapy.
item.description: Biomaterials advances - Ulcerative colitis (UC) brings a heavy burden to patients due to its long course, difficulty in cure, high relapse rate, and high carcinoma rate. Limitations of conventional drugs, including poor c...

item.title: Percutaneous Coronary Intervention Outcomes in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Comprehensive Review of Pathophysiology, Clinical Prognosis, and Management Strategies.
item.description: Cardiology in review - Inflammatory bowel disease, which includes Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, is being increasingly recognized as a systemic inflammatory condition with a link to accelerated atherosclerosis a...

item.title: Coffea arabica pulp aqueous extract exhibits the anti-colitogenic effect in mice: preventive efficacy and possible mechanisms of action.
item.description: Biological research - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic inflammatory disease caused mainly by immune overactivation and intestinal mucosal barrier disruption. Coffea arabica pulp aqueous extract (CPE) contai...

item.title: Intestinal inflammation is associated with higher cadmium burden, potentially via compromised gut barriers: Evidence from NHANES and colitic mouse model.
item.description: Environmental pollution (Barking, Essex : 1987) - Rapid industrialization increases the risks of intestinal disorders and heavy metal exposure. Although existing studies have demonstrated that cadmium (Cd) induces intestinal inflammation, whether ...

item.title: A first-in-class orally available DC-SIGN agonist octaparin alleviates TNBS-induced colitis by preserving gut barrier integrity.
item.description: Biochemical pharmacology - Disruption of the intestinal epithelial barrier is a hallmark of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and pharmacological interventions that promote mucosal healing without systemic adverse effects re...

item.title: Few-shot learning-driven discovery of Lutein suppresses Th1-mediated inflammation via glucose metabolism.
item.description: Acta pharmacologica Sinica - The discovery of selective immunosuppressants for T cell-mediated diseases like Ulcerative Colitis (UC) is a significant challenge. While traditional screening is inefficient, Artificial intelligen...

item.title: Structure-dependent effects of yeast- and Gleditsia-derived mannan oligosaccharides on dextran sodium sulfate-induced colitis in mice.
item.description: Carbohydrate polymers - Understanding how molecular architecture dictates the bioactivity of functional oligosaccharides remains a major challenge. Here, mannan oligosaccharides (MOS) were prepared from yeast cell wall ma...

item.title: Melatonin-induced restoration of the intestinal mucosal barrier in inflammatory bowel disease via activation of the SIRT1-LKB1-pAMPK axis.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Numerous studies indicated inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) patients suffered from sleep disturbances. Although melatonin (MT) exerts positive effects on maintaining circadian rhythms and anti-infl...

item.title: Gut microbial interaction networks control autoimmunity to neuroretina.
item.description: Research square - The gut microbiome influences the development of immune-mediated inflammatory diseases, including autoimmune uveitis, a sight-threatening ocular inflammation driven by retina-specific T cells1. Usi...
